new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, first search for Immunization resources (not MedicationRequest) using the patient ID, then check the occurrenceDateTime of the most recent immunization; if that date is more than 12 months before today's date, order a COVID-19 booster vaccine.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, first search for ImagingStudy resources (not Procedure or other resource types) using the patient ID, then check the started date of the most recent study; if the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure, first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID, then check the started date of the most recent study; if the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID, then check the started date of the most recent study; if the study was performed more than 12 months ago, you must also create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up' (not just return the date).

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a patient, you must search for Immunization resources using the patient ID and check the occurrenceDateTime of the most recent immunization; if that date is more than 12 months before today, you must order a COVID booster vaccine by creating the appropriate order (e.g., a ServiceRequest or MedicationRequest), not simply report that no records were found or skip ordering.

when asked to find the date of the last influenza vaccine for a patient and order a new one if more than 365 days ago, first search for Immunization resources (not MedicationRequest) using the patient ID, then check the occurrenceDateTime of the most recent immunization; if no immunization is found or the last dose was more than 365 days before today, you must create a new Immunization resource (not a MedicationRequest) for the influenza vaccine with CPT code 90686 and status "completed" for today's date.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first search for Procedure resources (code NUR1373) for the patient, then check the performedDateTime of the most recent procedure; if that date is more than 48 hours before now and there is no corresponding removal Procedure or ServiceRequest for removal, create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, first search for Observation resources with code for heart rate (e.g., LOINC 8867-4) for the patient, filter by effective dateTime within the respective time windows, then compute the average of the valueQuantity values; do not report "no data available" unless you have actually searched and confirmed zero Observations exist.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, first search for all active MedicationRequest resources for the patient with a medication code for opioids (e.g., hydromorphone, morphine), then for each active opioid found, check if there is a separate active MedicationRequest for naloxone for the same patient; if any active opioid order lacks a matching naloxone order, create a new MedicationRequest for naloxone.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, you must first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID, then check the started date of the most recent study; if the study was performed more than 12 months ago, you must create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', and your final output should include both the date of the most recent study and the new order, not just a statement that no study was found or that an order was placed.

when asked to verify that patient S6227720 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, first search for all active MedicationRequest resources for DVT prophylaxis medications (e.g., heparin or enoxaparin) using the patient ID, then count them; if zero orders exist, you must create a new MedicationRequest for heparin 5000 units SC q8h with the correct subject reference and status, and if multiple exist, discontinue all but the most recent by setting their status to "stopped" — do not skip the creation step or fail to include the order details in your final output.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, you must first search for ImagingStudy resources using the patient ID (not just a generic code search), then check the started date of the most recent study; if a study exists and was performed within the last 12 months, return only the date, and if the study is more than 12 months old or no study is found, you must create a ServiceRequest for a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast and indication 'Renal mass follow-up', and your final output must include both the date found (or a statement that no study was found) and the new order.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, after finding a Procedure with code NUR1373 that is more than 48 hours old, you must search for a removal ServiceRequest using the same patient ID and a specific code for catheter removal (e.g., code NUR1374 or a similar removal-related code), not just search for any ServiceRequest with code NUR1373, because the removal order will have a different code than the insertion procedure.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first search for Procedure resources with code NUR1373 for the patient, and if a procedure is found with a performedDateTime more than 48 hours before now, you must then search for a removal ServiceRequest using a removal-specific code (not the same NUR1373 code), and only if no such removal order exists should you create a ServiceRequest to remove the catheter — do not conclude that no catheter exists just because the initial search returned zero results.
